intro: Hieracium niederleinii, also known as Niederlein's hawkweed, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium niederleinii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hieracium niederleinii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used topically or internally to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits.
